## Verdict: REVISE

## Summary

Round 2's three findings (C-A, MAJ-A, MAJ-B) and two MINORs (min-A, min-B) are all RESOLVED in round 3 — verified by direct read of the plan and side-by-side comparison with the round-2 critic. The structural fix (D-07 + T-08 dynamic-glob) is well-designed and IS effective: an independent grep audit confirms it WOULD catch any future class-level omission. However, the round-3 audit-grep procedure that produced T-05's 11-row enumeration was itself incomplete in EXACTLY the same class-level pattern: an independent grep `<task-name>/(current-plan|critic-response|review-|gate-)` across `dev-workflow/skills/*/SKILL.md` finds 12 files, not 11. The 12th is `dev-workflow/skills/end_of_task/SKILL.md` line 71 (`<task-name>/review-*.md` lookup — load-bearing read of the latest review file at `/end_of_task` Step 1 pre-flight). This is the same shape as round-1 C1 (run + architect missed) and round-3 CRIT-A (rollback missed). The structural canary (T-09 grep (b)) WILL catch this at /implement time and produce a hard STOP, but the plan should preempt the catch by adding row 12 — same mechanical fix applied in round 3 for rollback.

## Issues

### Critical (blocks implementation)

(none)

### Major (significant gap, should address)

- **[MAJ-1] `end_of_task/SKILL.md` line 71 hardcodes `<task-name>/review-*.md`; missing from T-05's expanded 11-file edit list (third occurrence of the same class-level pattern)**
  - What: An independent grep `grep -rE '<task-name>/(current-plan|critic-response|review-|gate-)' dev-workflow/skills/*/SKILL.md` (the exact audit-grep that round-3 D-07 codifies as the source of truth for the file count) finds 12 files, not 11. The 12th is `dev-workflow/skills/end_of_task/SKILL.md:71`: `1. **Review status** — look for \`.workflow_artifacts/<task-name>/review-*.md\`. If no review file exists, STOP and tell the user: "No review found ..."`. This is a load-bearing read — `/end_of_task` Step 1 pre-flight uses it to find the latest review file and verify APPROVED verdict before proceeding to push + archive.
  - Why it matters: After stage-3 ships, when a user invokes `/end_of_task` for a stage-N sub-task (e.g., `stage 3 of quoin-foundation`), the `/review` skill (already in T-05's row 6) will write `review-<round>.md` into the resolved `<task-root>/stage-3/` subfolder. But `/end_of_task` Step 1 still looks at `<task-root>/<task-name>/review-*.md` — i.e., the task root, NOT the stage subfolder. The pre-flight check finds NO review file, STOPS with "No review found — please run `/review` first", and blocks the legitimate finalization. This is exactly the same class of orchestration-skill omission as round-1 C1 (run + architect) and round-3 CRIT-A (rollback) — the third occurrence in three rounds. Lesson 2026-04-13 (cross-skill SKILL.md edits) and Lesson 2026-04-14 (`/critic` should explicitly check all files referenced by name) both apply directly. The structural canary D-07/T-08 case (b) DOES catch this — the regex matches the line, end_of_task does NOT contain `path_resolve.py`, the assertion fails loudly with a clear diagnostic. T-09 grep (b) ALSO catches it (`grep -rn '<task-name>/critic-response\|<task-name>/review-\|<task-name>/gate-' dev-workflow/skills/` returns end_of_task line 71). Per the plan's own T-09 acceptance: "any unexpected match found in (a1), (a3), (b), or (c): /implement STOPS." So the plan-as-written produces a hard stop at T-09, the implementer must add row 12 to T-05, then re-run — exactly the same back-and-forth that round-3 CRIT-A's fix was supposed to preempt for rollback. The structural fix is working AS DESIGNED (loud failure, not silent breakage), but the plan can preempt the catch by adding the 12th row mechanically — same fix as round-3 CRIT-A.
  - Where: T-05 SKILL-edit task table (rows 1–11 → should be 1–12); T-07 verification procedure (currently reads only lines 199–230 of end_of_task/SKILL.md, missing line 71); T-09 grep (b) acceptance ("zero matches" — will fail without the fix); R-02 mitigation column ("11 SKILL.md files" — should be 12); session-state record. The plan's "## For human" claim "updates 11 SKILL.md files" should also bump to 12.
  - Suggestion: Apply the same mechanical fix as round-3 CRIT-A — extend T-05 from 11 to 12 rows. Add row 12 for `end_of_task/SKILL.md` with one anchor: line 71 (review-status pre-flight check). Per-file edit template applies as for the other 11 (resolver-prose with error-handling + D-03 carve-outs; the file ALSO contains a load-bearing line 145 `<task-name>/cost-ledger.md` reference which stays UNCHANGED per D-03 cost-ledger carve-out). Update T-07: instead of "no edit unless regression" (read-only verification of Step 7 archive logic at lines 199–230), expand T-07 to ALSO grep line 71 specifically AND confirm the resolver-prose lands there post-T-05. Update T-09 grep (d) lower bound from ≥11 to ≥12. Update T-08 sanity-floor assertion `len(SKILL_FILES) >= 11` to `>= 12` (or, preferably, leave at ≥11 as a true floor and let the conditional structural property carry the load). Update R-02 row text from "11 SKILL.md files (...round-3 added rollback...)" to "12 SKILL.md files (...round-3 added rollback; round-4 audit added end_of_task...)." Update the `## For human` `## Tasks` references from 11 to 12. Update the audit history in T-05's "why 11 not 8" prose to a four-step trajectory: 8 → 10 → 11 → 12. **Most importantly:** record in D-07 (or in a new D-09) that the round-3 audit-grep applied to T-05's enumeration was apparently run with a narrower regex than D-07's full alternation specifies — i.e., the round-3 audit found `<task-name>/current-plan` matches but did not run the full `<task-name>/(current-plan|critic-response|review-|gate-)` grep. Codify the explicit form of the audit-grep in D-07 with example output, so future maintainers run the FULL alternation, not a subset.

### Minor (improvement, use judgment)

- **[MIN-1] T-07 verification procedure does not cover end_of_task/SKILL.md line 71 (the review-file pre-flight check)**
  - Suggestion: T-07 currently reads lines 199–230 of `dev-workflow/skills/end_of_task/SKILL.md` (the Step 7 archive auto-detection block) and verifies the existing `current-plan.md`, `architecture.md`, `review-*.md`, `critic-response-*.md` enumeration is multi-stage-aware. But Step 1 pre-flight (line 71) uses a different code path that hardcodes the task-root path for `review-*.md` lookup — and T-07 does not read that. The MAJ-1 fix (add end_of_task to T-05) makes T-07 redundant for this specific anchor, but T-07 should still be expanded to grep ALL `<task-name>/` references in end_of_task/SKILL.md (currently 3: lines 71, 145, 205) and explicitly classify each (line 71 → resolver edit per MAJ-1; line 145 → cost-ledger carve-out per D-03; line 205 → `finalized/<task-name>/` archive parent — D-03 corollary, no edit). Acceptance: T-07 closes with all three lines explicitly classified in the session-state "Decisions made" section.

- **[MIN-2] Sanity-floor assertion `len(SKILL_FILES) >= 11` in T-08 will fail-LATE on a typo/cwd error**
  - Suggestion: Cosmetic. The sanity floor is correct in principle (catches a glob that returns 0 due to wrong cwd) but the diagnostic message says "expected ≥ 11 after T-05 (round-3 CRIT-A)" — which becomes stale when MAJ-1 bumps the count to 12. After applying MAJ-1, either bump the floor to ≥12 OR rephrase the diagnostic to: "expected ≥ 11 after T-05 (informational lower bound dated 2026-04-26 per D-07; the actual count is determined by the audit-grep procedure, not this floor)". The latter is more durable across future stage additions. The plan's existing D-07 framing ("T-05's row count is an INFORMATIONAL audit snapshot dated 2026-04-26") matches the latter phrasing better.

## Notes

### What's good (preserve in round 4 if any)

- The verbatim line-116 rewrite (MAJ-A fix) is well-shaped: three-bullet split with explicit `<task-root>` vs `<task_dir>` placeholders, exit-2 fallback prose, implementer notes preventing off-by-one. The rewrite is implementable as-is by `/implement` without further interpretation.
- D-07's three-component formulation (audit-grep procedure + test-side enforcement + plan-side documentation) is the right structure. The "Why this is a Decision, not just a Note" rationale block is unusually well-justified — it explicitly cites three rounds of the same class-level omission as evidence the failure is structural, not contingent. This is exactly the meta-finding the round-2 critic asked for.
- The dynamic-glob conversion of T-08 case (b) and (c) is exemplary: a conditional structural property ("IF hardcoded path THEN path_resolve.py") that catches future omissions automatically without test-code edits. The carve-out comment for architecture.md / architecture-critic-N.md / cost-ledger.md is correct (the regex restricts to `(current-plan|critic-response|review-|gate-)` which excludes those three filenames natively).
- The deviation flag in round-2 revision history (plan/SKILL.md line 16 anchor — verified correct via direct read; round-1 critic was wrong) is preserved correctly through round 3. This is the lesson 2026-04-26 deviation-from-critic-suggestion pattern applied properly.
- D-08's positive-Decision form for the SYNC WARNING question (replacing Q-03's deferred "plan assumes") is the right resolution. The T-05 acceptance grep is the canary against drift.
- Production-shape verification (round-2 CRIT-2) is preserved and extended: T-04 case (s) reads `_inflight-snapshot.txt` and hard-asserts each in-flight folder's live state matches the snapshot. T-08 case (e) does per-folder shape fingerprint independent assertions. The snapshot file IS the contract per lesson 2026-04-22 fixture-stability.
- T-10's three explicit numeric abort criteria (90s wall-clock, $1.00 cost, phase-creep substring detection) and `verified-with-abort` outcome are preserved. The cost-discipline framing matches the architecture's lesson 2026-04-22 anti-target.

### Loop-detection signal

Round-1 issue titles: C1 "/run and /architect SKILL.md missing"; C2 "mixed/ fixture mismatch"; C3 "anchor-line precision"; M1–M6 + m1–m3.

Round-2 issue titles: C-A "rollback/SKILL.md ALSO hardcodes plan path"; MAJ-A "gate/SKILL.md line 116 rewrite promised but not provided"; MAJ-B "T-05's audit-method gap repeats round-1 C1's incompleteness".

Round-3 NEW issue title: MAJ-1 "end_of_task/SKILL.md line 71 hardcodes plan path; missing from T-05's expanded 11-file edit list."

**Same-CLASS reappearance, third occurrence.** Round-1 C1 (run + architect missed) → round-2 C-A (rollback missed) → round-3 MAJ-1 (end_of_task missed). Three distinct skills missed across three rounds — the recurring pattern that round 3 was explicitly trying to close. **Crucially, however:** the round-3 structural fix (D-07 + T-08 case (b)/(c) dynamic glob) IS effective at catching this 12th occurrence — verified by independent grep + reading T-08 case (b)'s assertion logic. The recurrence is at the static-enumeration level (T-05's row count), not at the structural-contract level (T-08 catches what T-05 misses). This is exactly the convergence trajectory the round-2 critic predicted: "the structural test makes the static enumeration anti-pattern moot." The round-3 fix WORKS; the only remaining gap is that T-05's row count doesn't match the audit-grep's actual output (11 vs 12).

**Severity downgrade rationale.** Round 1 was 3 CRIT + 6 MAJ + 3 MIN (12 issues). Round 2 was 1 CRIT + 2 MAJ + 2 MIN (5 issues). Round 3 is 0 CRIT + 1 MAJ + 2 MIN (3 issues). Strict decrease in count + severity continues. The round-3 finding is mechanical (add row 12 to T-05; same shape as round-3 CRIT-A's rollback addition). The structural canary catches it pre-merge regardless. Recommend ONE more revision pass to clear MAJ-1 + MIN-1 + MIN-2; if round-4 produces no new findings AND no same-title-or-class repeat, advance to /implement.

**Class-level recurrence note (for lessons-learned).** This is the third round in which an additional SKILL.md was found to hardcode a planning-artifact path that was missed by the prior round's audit. The round-3 fix (D-07 + dynamic glob) is the correct structural response to this pattern. After round-4 lands MAJ-1, write a lessons-learned entry: "When introducing a path-resolution dispatch convention across multiple skills, manual enumeration of affected SKILL.md files is unreliable for >5 files; use a dynamic glob + conditional structural assertion (`IF file contains hardcoded pattern THEN file MUST contain resolver reference`) and let the test enforce completeness, not the plan author." This is the lesson the round-3 plan author tried to encode in D-07 but did not yet apply to D-07's own audit-grep execution.

### New-issue calibration

Round 3 produced 1 MAJ + 2 MIN. Round 2 produced 1 CRIT + 2 MAJ + 2 MIN. Round 1 produced 3 CRIT + 6 MAJ + 3 MIN. The trajectory matches the convergence regime for a Large-profile task: each round's findings are weakly subordinate to the prior round's fixes. Round 3's MAJ-1 is mechanical (add row 12), MIN-1 is a tightening of T-07 scope, MIN-2 is a one-line diagnostic-message tweak. None require a redesign. Round-4 verdict prediction: PASS likely if MAJ-1 is addressed (whether by adding row 12 OR by explicitly accepting the row-count-vs-audit-grep gap and trusting T-09 to catch it). The plan has good bones; one more pass should converge.

### Calibration vs round-2 critic's PASS-prediction

Round-2 critic predicted: "Round-3 verdict prediction: PASS likely if C-A + MAJ-A are addressed AND MAJ-B's dynamic-enumeration suggestion is adopted." Round 3 addressed all three. The PASS prediction would be correct EXCEPT the round-3 audit-grep that produced T-05's row count was incomplete in the same class-level pattern that MAJ-B was meant to fix. The structural test catches the gap, so the integration-safety story is intact — but a clean PASS verdict requires the static enumeration in the plan to match the audit-grep that the plan itself codifies as the source of truth. The MAJ-1 fix is one mechanical row addition + one D-07 clarification.

| Correctness | good | Anchor lines re-verified for the round-3 addition (rollback line 53 + 65 confirmed by direct read); production fixture shapes still match real folders byte-for-byte; resolver semantics complete (rule-1/2a/2b/2c/2d/3 enumeration + CLI exit codes); MAJ-A verbatim rewrite is implementable. |
| Integration safety | good | D-03 + D-03 corollary (architecture-critic-N.md task-root) explicitly carved out at every test site; T-09 (b) WILL catch end_of_task line 71 pre-merge (loud failure, not silent breakage); existing in-flight task folders preserved per D-02. |
| Risk coverage | good | R-01..R-10 each map to a test or grep; R-09's snapshot-based hard-assert closes silent-skip; R-03's multi-match raise closes silent-route; R-02's audit-grep procedure (D-07) closes the manual-enumeration drift in principle (though not yet in practice — MAJ-1). |
| Testability | good | 22 unit cases + 7 e2e cases; case (s) and case (e) fail loudly; T-08 case (b)/(c) dynamic glob is the structural canary against future SKILL.md additions. The dynamic glob WILL fail on end_of_task during /implement, surfacing MAJ-1 mechanically. |
| Implementability | fair | MAJ-1 will produce a hard stop at T-09 (b); the implementer must back up to T-05, add row 12, re-run. This is recoverable but causes a back-and-forth loop that round-3 CRIT-A's fix was supposed to preempt for rollback (and analogous fix should preempt for end_of_task). MIN-1 (T-07 scope) compounds: T-07 currently doesn't grep line 71, so the regression is not caught until T-09. |
| De-risking | good | T-10 cost cap concrete (3 numeric criteria); fixture-first ordering preserved; SKILL-edit gated on T-04 PASS; D-07 audit-grep procedure documented as a permanent guardrail (with the MAJ-1 caveat that the audit-grep was not yet applied to round-3's own enumeration). |
